creator: tcberner@freebsd.org
text:
Moin moin

I'm using a pair of USB KEF X300A speakers. Which were working perfetly fine [2].


Since a an upgrade of current [1] recently, they fail hover to trigger the creation of an audio device

When I plug them in, I get the following message

Sep  1 14:20:29 angua kernel: usbd_setup_device_desc: getting device descriptor at addr 2 failed, USB_ERR_IOERROR
Sep  1 14:20:35 angua syslogd: last message repeated 4 times
Sep  1 14:20:35 angua kernel: ugen2.3: <Unknown > at usbus2 (disconnected)
Sep  1 14:20:35 angua kernel: uhub_reattach_port: could not allocate new device




mfg Tobias

[1] FreeBSD angua.firefly 13.0-CURRENT FreeBSD 13.0-CURRENT #77 r364916: Sat Aug 29 21:38:50 CEST 2020     Tobias@angua.firefly:/usr/obj/usr/src/amd64.amd64/sys/ANGUA  amd64
[2] Testing them on an other system confirmed that they are still working.
